name: Iceland (proposed flag, 1914)
description: A white field charged with a Nordic cross of two shades of blue — a wider light-blue cross with a narrower medium-blue inner stripe.
history: One of the alternative designs proposed during the Icelandic flag debate of 1913–1915 — featuring two shades of blue on a white field. It was among the candidates considered alongside the eventually-adopted blue/white/red design.
